prose: Fukui reactivity indices for phenol were computed at the DFT level using the B3LYP/def2-TZVP functional/basis set (tier 'standard'), in the gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1, via PySCF (chemkit fukui skill). Exact four-centre integrals were used (no density fitting). The geometry was taken directly from the user-supplied phenol.xyz without re-optimisation. Three SCF calculations were run (neutral, cation N−1 charge +1 mult 2, anion N+1 charge −1 mult 2); Mulliken partial charges were used to derive the Fukui indices. All three SCFs converged. Integrity checks passed (sum(f+) = 1.0000, sum(f−) = 1.0000); integrity.trustworthy = true, status = ok. No warnings were reported. The headline value reported is the maximum f+ (most electrophilic site): f+ = 2.1028319 on C3 (atom index 3, ortho carbon relative to OH). The most nucleophilic site is O1 (f− = 0.16201477). Dual descriptor analysis confirms ortho/para electrophilic character and nucleophilic character at the oxygen, consistent with phenol's known reactivity in electrophilic aromatic substitution. Output JSON: phenol_fukui_dft_b3lyp_def2tzvp.json. CLI: /home/erobles/chem-skills/mcp_server/chemkit_engine/cli.py fukui /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/phenol/phenol.xyz --method dft --tier standard --functional b3lyp --basis def2-tzvp --charge 0 --mult 1.
